rationale: The signals show very broad, generic search demand (Google autocomplete is dominated by "crested gecko" + care/husbandry terms like enclosure, diet, lifespan). Bluesky is mostly fan art and general pet content, not breeder-specific discussion. YouTube has significant watch-through on morph guides and cappuccino debate, but all the high-view content is already 1–8 years old, indicating saturated coverage. No forum heat, no recent breeder blogs, no trending topics. The only signal with slightly fresher debate is cappuccino geckos (some conflicting takes in recent YT titles like "Huge Mistake or Hidden Opportunity?" and "Why So Much Hate?"), but we already have three cappuccino-related slugs published. Nothing clears the originality bar or shows concrete breeder confusion that warrants a post. Rejecting all topics.
